headline: Durian Exports to China Surge 340% to RM8.2B
context: Malaysia's durian exports to China exploded 340% to RM8.2 billion in 2025 following the fresh durian export protocol agreement, making Musang King and Black Thorn premium varieties Malaysia's fastest-growing agricultural export.

cards.big: Durian exports hit RM8.2 billion — Musang King now earns more foreign exchange than rubber for the first time
cards.sub: China's demand for Malaysian durian grew 340% in one year after fresh export protocol was finalised

cards.big: Single-market dependency at 94% of exports going to China creates vulnerability if trade relations sour
cards.sub: Durian price at RM80/kg in China versus RM35/kg domestic — export diversion is pushing local prices up 28%

cards.big: Pahang's 22,000 durian smallholders saw income triple but land prices soared — pricing out new entrants and food farmers
cards.sub: Agricultural land conversion from rice and vegetables to durian orchards reduced food production in 3 states

cards.big: MAQIS certification for China export compliance has 14-month backlog — only 186 of 480 applicant farms approved
cards.sub: Phytosanitary testing capacity at 3 labs is insufficient — some farmers bypass official channels through grey market exports

cards.big: Durian is a genuine success story — but putting 94% of it in one basket called China is a risky bet Malaysia has made before
Should Malaysia cap durian exports at 70% of production to maintain domestic affordability and market diversification?
